must_detect.matched_text:
The event disrupted our EU asset monitoring platform for onshore wind sites, with preliminary internal revenue impact estimated at €4.8–€6.2m for Q3 (non‑public, subject to validation).
We assess this as inside information under EU MAR (Article 7) and have decided to delay disclosure under Article 17(4) given ongoing containment and a legitimate interest in avoiding prejudice to negotiations with our insurer.
Dissemination via an approved information service will occur without delay once confidentiality can no longer be ensured or on/before 14 June 2026, in line with AME Issuer Guidance 5.2.
Selective disclosure controls are in place; no briefing of analysts or suppliers is permitted, and any market sounding will require wall‑crossing and inclusion on the insider list (liste d’initiés).
During the current PDMR closed period (blackout window) preceding our H1 results announcement planned for 30 July 2026, all PDMR dealings remain prohibited except under MAR Article 19(12) exemptions.
The incident originated from a compromised third‑country processor (CirrusLyte Pte Ltd, SG) used for log aggregation; cross‑border transfers rely on SCCs with a documented TIA; emergency routing to EU‑only processors is underway.
Affected datasets include limited HR files for 212 staff (employee IDs VR‑44721—VR‑44903, emails, phone numbers) and a subset containing “health leave category” and works‑council affiliation, triggering GDPR Article 9 processing safeguards.
No payment card or government ID data are involved; operational control systems were not breached, according to current forensic telemetry.
Data subjects to be notified under GDPR Articles 33–34 within 72 hours; Data Protection Officer Mr. Adrien Kovacs (dpo@verdantis‑renew.eu) is coordinating with the competent supervisory authority.
Per CSRD internal control policies, the temporary loss of metering inputs may affect Scope 2 KPIs and SFDR PAI indicators for Q2; if metrics change by >5%, this may be material for issuer‑specific sustainability targets.
